chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 479 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.2940/2025. 2. For orders on office objection No.22. 3. For orders on CMA No.2591/2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 11.02.2025 Mr. Muzaffar Ali Dehraj, advocate for the petitioner. 1. Urgency granted. 2to4. Matter pertains to CNIC and office objection 4 denote as to how this petition is maintainable in view of order dated 27.02.2023 passed in CP D 6944 of 2022. Prima facie, the remedy before the Grievance Commissioner is not exhausted, hence, direct recourse to writ is unwarranted. Even otherwise, attention is drawn to page 53 where upon the consideration to documentation the petitioner was found to be disentitled. Per counsel since no further provision of appeal is available against the said order, hence, recourse to writ. This plea is also misconceived in view of the judgment of the Supreme Court passed in Gul Taiz Khan Marwat vs. The Registrar, Peshawar High Court, Peshawar (PLD 2021 SC 391). In view hereof no case is made out, hence, this petition is dismissed in limine alongwith pending applications. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
